questions.question: The bystander effect occurs primarily because of:

options.text: Diffusion of responsibility and pluralistic ignorance
Fundamental attribution error and actor-observer bias

questions.explanation: The bystander effect operates through diffusion of responsibility (each person feels less personally responsible when others are present) and pluralistic ignorance (bystanders look to each other for cues and each concludes the situation is not an emergency).

questions.question: According to Batson's empathy-altruism hypothesis, truly altruistic helping behavior is most likely when a person:

options.text: Feels genuine empathic concern for the person in distress
Has a personality high in extraversion
Expects to receive a reward for helping

questions.explanation: Batson's empathy-altruism hypothesis proposes that when people feel genuine empathic concern for another person, they are motivated to help regardless of personal benefit, representing truly altruistic motivation.
